reasoning:
1) Baseline Analysis: The 360-day baseline (Oct 2024–Oct 2025) explicitly excludes the massive July-August 2024 student protest massacres, landing at an average of 31.167 ACLED-counted fatalities per 30-day period (roughly 1 death per day from typical interim-era political/mob violence). 
2) Period 1 (30 days before 2025-11-02): Rights groups reported a sharp deterioration in security during October 2025, with 13 custodial deaths and 66 unidentified bodies recovered. While ACLED may heavily filter unidentified bodies, the noted spike in mob justice makes exceeding the ~31 baseline reasonably likely here. 
3) Period 2 (30 days before 2025-11-25): The death sentence for Sheikh Hasina and her interior minister on Nov 17, 2025 predictably triggered Awami League protests, transportation shutdowns, and several dozen bombings. While widespread "casualties" weren't conclusively confirmed in initial reporting, the unrest likely kept ACLED-recorded fatalities over the normal baseline.
4) Period 3 (30 days before 2026-01-24): This specific window (Dec 25 - Jan 23) narrowly misses the severe Dec 18-20 riots over the assassination of a youth leader. Human rights monitoring noted that Jan 2026 saw only 4 political deaths (up from 1 in Dec 2025). The intense police crackdown ahead of the February national elections seems to have kept political fatalities low, meaning this period is likely closer to or below the baseline. Following instructions, the probabilities converge towards 0.50 for the most distant horizon.
